creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
As it turns out I have one, USB ID b58e:9e84

ugen0.7: <Blue Microphones Yeti Stereo Microphone> at usbus0, cfg=0 md=HOST spd=FULL (12Mbps) pwr=ON (100mA)

I tried w/o virtual_oss. Recording and playback via Audacity worked fine, choosing /dev/dsp2. Setting sysctl hw.snd.default_unit=2 and then playing a Youtube video in Firefox worked fine.

ugen0.7: <Blue Microphones Yeti Stereo Microphone> at usbus0
uaudio0 on uhub0
uaudio0: <Blue Microphones Yeti Stereo Microphone, class 0/0, rev 1.10/1.00, addr 10> on usbus0
uaudio0: Play[0]: 48000 Hz, 2 ch, 16-bit S-LE PCM format, 2x2ms buffer.
uaudio0: Play[0]: 44100 Hz, 2 ch, 16-bit S-LE PCM format, 2x2ms buffer.
uaudio0: Record[0]: 48000 Hz, 2 ch, 16-bit S-LE PCM format, 2x2ms buffer.
uaudio0: Record[0]: 44100 Hz, 2 ch, 16-bit S-LE PCM format, 2x2ms buffer.
uaudio0: No MIDI sequencer.
pcm2: <USB audio> on uaudio0
uaudio0: HID volume keys found.

The kernel on this test laptop is main from August 2023 plus a bunch of work in progress (but I believe nothing significant related to audio).

This device has a mute button, presumably responsible for the "HID volume keys found." message -- I note this is different from what yours reported.
